DECLARATION OF LAWRENCE A. WEISS IN SUPPORT OF JAMES RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 68223782) FILED BY RESPONDENT JAMES RIVER INSURANCE COMPANY
MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-19-2022 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PARTICIPATE IN BINDING ARBITRATON AND FOR SANCTINS AGAINST L. WEISS IS GRANTED. THE PARTIES ARE OBLIGATED TO ARBITRATE THIS DISPUTE PER INSURANCE CODE SEC. 11580.2(F). DEFENDANT'S MERITS ARGUMENTS SHALL BE DIRECTED TO THE ARBITRATOR. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE APPOINTMENT OF AN ARBITRATOR BY OCTOBER 31, 2022. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON AN ARBITRATOR, THEY SHALL EACH PROVIDE FIVE PROPOSED ARBITRATORS TO THE COURT IN A JOINT EX PARTE APPLICATION BY NOVEMBER 10, 2022, AND THE COURT WILL FOLLOW THE PROCESS OF CCP 1281.6. THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THE PARTIES SHOULD BE ABLE TO RESOLVE THIS ISSUE WITHOUT FURTHER COURT INTERVENTION. THE COURT DENIES SANCTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: RAQUEL SHARP, CSR# 10619, (925.384.0003; RAQUEL@TRANSCRIPTLEGAL.COM), REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PARTICIPATE IN BINDING ARBITRATON AND FOR SANCTINS AGAINST L. WEISS
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
David Hart, Esq. (650.355.3000) for Petitioner.
Bryan Stofferahn, Esq. (510.540.4881; bstofferahn@bremerwhyte.com) of Bremer Whyte Brown & O'Meara LLP for Respondent.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO PARTICIPATE IN BINDING ARBITRATON AND FOR SANCTINS AGAINST L. WEISS.

Plaintiff's motion to compel arbitration is granted. The parties are obligated to arbitrate this dispute per Insurance Code sec. 11580.2(f). Defendant's merits arguments shall be directed to the arbitrator. The parties shall meet and confer regarding the appointment of an arbitrator by October 31, 2022. If the parties cannot agree on an arbitrator, they shall each provide five proposed arbitrators to the court in a joint ex parte application by November 10, 2022, and the court will follow the process of CCP 1281.6. The court believes that the parties should be able to resolve this issue without further court intervention. The court denies sanctions.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Raquel Sharp, CSR# 10619, (925.384.0003; raquel@transcriptlegal.com), Reported. =(302/RBU)
